frontend/src/components/ShortpickLabView.reason: Short Pick Lab workspace component currently owns live run, validation queue, model feedback, historical replay workbench state, and the strategy closeout readout in one Ant Design surface. Historical replay now includes a reduced non-expert default readout, aggregate statistical readouts, per-run drilldown, readable replay family labels, explicit loading states for slow replay feedback tables, and the frozen paper strategy summary with market-factor controls, multi-index references, cost stress, and paper-tracking status. Heavy replay aggregate/per-run feedback and strategy closeout data now load in the background instead of blocking initial lab or replay navigation. Live runs now surface the frozen paper strategy separately from LLM and market-factor control groups; the paper-tracking tab explains the four trading-day exit tracks, the strict daily LLM paper-control stock selected from the LLM recommendation pool, and same-pool market controls including offensive Top1, cooldown Top1, and deterministic random baseline. The no-live-run fallback now defaults to paper tracking without locking users out of replay/history tabs. It should be split into tab components after replay contracts stabilize, but keeping it together avoids cross-file churn during this visible replay closeout. 2026-05-11 review: growth comes from frozen paper-strategy closeout, explicit loading-state contracts, market-rule filtering, and paper-tracking/backtest comparison surfaces; split remains deferred until the experiment contract stops changing.

tests/test_shortpick_lab.py.reason: Short Pick Lab contract tests cover isolated executor behavior, DeepSeek search planning and fallback search, source integrity, candidate parsing, validation status handling, live strategy overlay invariants, frozen paper-tracking contract rules, LLM paper-control selection, market-factor paper controls, four-track exit snapshot calculation, and display-horizon consistency for paper-tracked market controls. Kept together while the shortpick executor contract is still changing rapidly. 2026-05-11 review: growth comes from frozen paper-strategy closeout, explicit loading-state contracts, market-rule filtering, and paper-tracking/backtest comparison surfaces; split remains deferred until the experiment contract stops changing.

src/ashare_evidence/shortpick_lab.py.reason: Short Pick Lab workflow: native-web/open-discovery execution, source integrity, candidate parsing, validation, retry, serialization, and model feedback. Live runs now attach the frozen v3 paper-tracking strategy as a first-class candidate when its market-positive/not-overheated gate passes, mark exactly one LLM paper-control candidate per run via a frozen deterministic ranking rule, and add same-pool market controls for offensive Top1, cooldown Top1, and deterministic random Top40 selection. The frozen, LLM paper-control, and market-control validation payloads record mechanical 5-trading-day, mechanical 10-trading-day, conditional 5-to-10-day, and 10% take-profit exit tracks once the 10-day window is complete; paper-tracked market controls also use a 10-trading-day display horizon while ordinary non-tracked market candidates remain 5-day controls. The overlay refreshes the existing research universe only during run generation and commits per symbol to avoid long SQLite locks. DeepSeek search uses local SearXNG first and a public Sogou fallback when local engines return no usable results, while retaining fail-closed source enforcement. 2026-05-11 review: growth also includes a production guard that applies an in-process 180-second soft timeout to each DeepSeek+search shortpick round, so one slow external round is marked failed without blocking the whole scheduled batch or killing the Python process. A module split remains desirable after the API contract stabilizes; deferred here to keep the live-facing incident fix narrowly scoped.
